records.ety: [F. chute. See Chute. Confused with shoot to let fly.]

defs: An inclined plane, either artificial or natural, down which timber, coal, etc., are caused to slide; also, a narrow passage, either natural or artificial, in a stream, where the water rushes rapidly; esp., a channel, having a swift current, connecting the ends of a bend in the stream, so as to shorten the course.

records.ety: [OE. shotien, schotien, AS. scotian, v. i., scetan; akin to D. schieten, G. schie<?/en, OHG. sciozan, Icel. skj<?/ta, Sw. skjuta, Dan. skyde; cf. Skr. skund to jump. √159. Cf. Scot a contribution, Scout to reject, Scud, Scuttle, v. i., Shot, Sheet, Shut, Shuttle, Skittish, Skittles.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Shot (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Shooting. The old participle Shotten is obsolete. See Shotten.]

defs: To let fly, or cause to be driven, with force, as an arrow or a bullet; -- followed by a word denoting the missile, as an object.
quotes: If you please To shoot an arrow that self way.

defs: To discharge, causing a missile to be driven forth; -- followed by a word denoting the weapon or instrument, as an object; -- often with off; as, to shoot a gun.
quotes: The two ends od a bow, shot off, fly from one another.

defs: To strike with anything shot; to hit with a missile; often, to kill or wound with a firearm; -- followed by a word denoting the person or thing hit, as an object.
quotes: When Roger shot the hawk hovering over his master's dove house.

defs: To send out or forth, especially with a rapid or sudden motion; to cast with the hand; to hurl; to discharge; to emit.
quotes: An honest weaver as ever shot shuttle.
A pit into which the dead carts had nightly shot corpses by scores.

defs: To push or thrust forward; to project; to protrude; -- often with out; as, a plant shoots out a bud.
quotes: They shoot out the lip, they shake the head.
Beware the secret snake that shoots a sting.

defs: To plane straight; to fit by planing.
quotes: Two pieces of wood that are shot, that is, planed or else pared with a paring chisel.

defs: To pass rapidly through, over, or under; as, to shoot a rapid or a bridge; to shoot a sand bar.
quotes: She . . . shoots the Stygian sound.

defs: To variegate as if by sprinkling or intermingling; to color in spots or patches.
quotes: The tangled water courses slept, Shot over with purple, and green, and yellow.

defs: To cause an engine or weapon to discharge a missile; -- said of a person or an agent; as, they shot at a target; he shoots better than he rides.
quotes: The archers have . . . shot at him.

defs: To discharge a missile; -- said of an engine or instrument; as, the gun shoots well.
To be shot or propelled forcibly; -- said of a missile; to be emitted or driven; to move or extend swiftly, as if propelled; as, a shooting star.
quotes: There shot a streaming lamp along the sky.

defs: To penetrate, as a missile; to dart with a piercing sensation; as, shooting pains.
quotes: Thy words shoot through my heart.

defs: To feel a quick, darting pain; to throb in pain.
quotes: These preachers make His head to shoot and ache.

defs: To germinate; to bud; to sprout.
quotes: Onions, as they hang, will shoot forth.
But the wild olive shoots, and shades the ungrateful plain.

defs: To grow; to advance; as, to shoot up rapidly.
quotes: Well shot in years he seemed.
Delightful task! to rear the tender thought, To teach the young idea how to shoot.

defs: To change form suddenly; especially, to solidify.
quotes: If the menstruum be overcharged, metals will shoot into crystals.

defs: To protrude; to jut; to project; to extend; as, the land shoots into a promontory.
quotes: There shot up against the dark sky, tall, gaunt, straggling houses.

defs: To move ahead by force of momentum, as a sailing vessel when the helm is put hard alee.
The act of shooting; the discharge of a missile; a shot; as, the shoot of a shuttle.
quotes: The Turkish bow giveth a very forcible shoot.
One underneath his horse to get a shoot doth stalk.

defs: A young branch or growth.
quotes: Superfluous branches and shoots of this second spring.

defs: A rush of water; a rapid.
A vein of ore running in the same general direction as the lode.
A weft thread shot through the shed by the shuttle; a pick.
A shoat; a young hog.
